title: × serapicamptis andaluciana
intro: × Serapicamptis andaluciana, also known as the Andalusian Serapias, is a hybrid orchid species native to the Iberian Peninsula. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Spanish and Portuguese folk medicine, where it is valued for its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of × Serapicamptis andaluciana include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made by infusing its flowers and leaves in water, which can be used to create teas, infusions, or applied topically as a poultice.
